title: Bump 1.25.2
body:
As the title says
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

The bump to containerd slipped in, not sure if we need to get it or not.
@rhatdan @flouthoc @Luap99 PTAL
danger zone: we're cutting this from the main branch.  Dependabot will continue bumping dependencies which in turn makes potential backports for RHEL harder.

@TomSweeneyRedHat I think new containerd bump https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3957 adds fixes for `CNI and selinux`  but I don't think it is affecting us If we could get that commit it would be good though cause its already on upstream but not a blocker afaics . @Luap99 could you also take a look if we would need this `containerd` bump from CNI perspective in new release.
https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3957 changed exactly 0 code under /vendor so there is 0 risk with that PR.
@TomSweeneyRedHat We don't need containerd PR so this good.

/lgtm
Lets just bump the release number of the --dev branch on main and create 1.25 branch.

/hold
BTW This should be 1.26 we added new features.
Lets change this PR to v1.26.0 and then move main branch to v1.27.0-dev, create a v1.26 branch.
Closing, per @rhatdan we need to bump to 1.26
